records.ety: [OE. sterven to die, AS. steorfan; akin to D. sterven, G. sterben, OHG. sterban, Icel. starf labor, toil.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Starved (stärvd); p. pr. & vb. n. Starving.]

quotes: In hot coals he hath himself raked . . . Thus starved this worthy mighty Hercules.

defs: To perish with hunger; to suffer extreme hunger or want; to be very indigent.
quotes: Sometimes virtue starves, while vice is fed.

defs: To perish or die with cold.
quotes: Have I seen the naked starve for cold?
Starving with cold as well as hunger.
notes: ☞ In this sense, still common in England, but rarely used in the United States.

quotes: From beds of raging fire, to starve in ice Their soft ethereal warmth.

defs: To kill with hunger; as, maliciously to starve a man is, in law, murder.
To distress or subdue by famine; as, to starve a garrison into a surrender.
quotes: Attalus endeavored to starve Italy by stopping their convoy of provisions from Africa.

defs: To destroy by want of any kind; as, to starve plants by depriving them of proper light and air.
To deprive of force or vigor; to disable.
quotes: The pens of historians, writing thereof, seemed starved for matter in an age so fruitful of memorable actions.
The powers of their minds are starved by disuse.
